name: pam
description: Linux-PAM API contracts — verified ground truth for PAM internals

contracts.behavior: _pam_drop(X) calls free(X) then sets X = NULL. The caller's pointer is NULLed after free; subsequent use of that variable is a NULL-deref crash (not exploitable UAF). Double-free is also prevented since free(NULL) is a no-op.

pam_set_item() deep-copies its data argument (strdup for strings, memcpy for structures). The PAM handle holds an independent copy. Freeing or overwriting the original buffer does not create a dangling reference inside the PAM handle.
pam_set_data() copies the data_name key string (strdup). Freeing or reusing the caller's key buffer does not corrupt PAM's internal data registry.
pam_read_passwords() bounds all reads to PAM_MAX_RESP_SIZE (512 bytes). Input from the conversation function is length-checked before copy.
pam_modutil_write() loops write() until all bytes are written or an error occurs. It handles partial writes correctly — short-write vulnerabilities do not apply.

D() is a debug-only macro that expands to nothing unless compiled with -DDEBUG. In production builds (the default), D() arguments are never evaluated. Format-string bugs, information leaks, or side effects inside D() are not reachable in production.
